{
  "generation": 1,
  "nodes": 14,
  "status": "ok",
  "text": "func CreateNewAccountCli(t testing.TB, loginName string) (string, string) {
n1: 	c, err := loadConfig()
n2: 	require.NoError(t, err)

n3: 	ctx := context.Background()
n4: 	password, err := base62.Random(16)
n5: 	require.NoError(t, err)
n6: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs(
			"accounts", "create", "password",
			"-auth-method-id", c.AuthMethodId,
			"-login-name", loginName,
			"-password", "env://E2E_TEST_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD",
			"-name", "e2e Account "+loginName,
			"-description", "e2e Account",
			"-format", "json",
		),
		e2e.WithEnv("E2E_TEST_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD", password),
	)
n7: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))

n8: 	var newAccountResult accounts.AccountCreateResult
n9: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newAccountResult)
n10: 	require.NoError(t, err)

n11: 	newAccountId := newAccountResult.Item.Id
n12: 	t.Cleanup(func() {
		AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
		output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
			e2e.WithArgs("accounts", "delete", "-id", newAccountId),
		)
		require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
	})

n13: 	t.Logf("Created Account: %s", newAccountId)

n14: 	return newAccountId, password
}
"
}

{
  "generation": 1,
  "nodes": 4,
  "status": "ok",
  "text": "func AuthenticateCli(t testing.TB, loginName string, password string) {
n1: 	c, err := loadConfig()
n2: 	require.NoError(t, err)

n3: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(context.Background(), "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs(
			"authenticate", "password",
			"-addr", c.Address,
			"-auth-method-id", c.AuthMethodId,
			"-login-name", loginName,
			"-password", "env://E2E_TEST_BOUNDARY_PASSWORD",
		),
		e2e.WithEnv("E2E_TEST_BOUNDARY_PASSWORD", password),
	)
n4: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
}
"
}

{
  "generation": 1,
  "nodes": 57,
  "status": "ok",
  "text": "func TestSessionCancelUserCli(t *testing.T) {
n1: 	e2e.MaybeSkipTest(t)
n2: 	c, err := loadConfig()
n3: 	require.NoError(t, err)

n4: 	boundary.AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
n5: 	newOrgId := boundary.CreateNewOrgCli(t)
n6: 	newProjectId := boundary.CreateNewProjectCli(t, newOrgId)
n7: 	newHostCatalogId := boundary.CreateNewHostCatalogCli(t, newProjectId)
n8: 	newHostSetId := boundary.CreateNewHostSetCli(t, newHostCatalogId)
n9: 	newHostId := boundary.CreateNewHostCli(t, newHostCatalogId, c.TargetIp)
n10: 	boundary.AddHostToHostSetCli(t, newHostSetId, newHostId)
n11: 	newTargetId := boundary.CreateNewTargetCli(t, newProjectId, c.TargetPort)
n12: 	boundary.AddHostSourceToTargetCli(t, newTargetId, newHostSetId)
n13: 	acctName := "e2e-account"
n14: 	newAccountId, acctPassword := boundary.CreateNewAccountCli(t, acctName)
n15: 	newUserId := boundary.CreateNewUserCli(t, "global")
n16: 	boundary.SetAccountToUserCli(t, newUserId, newAccountId)

	// Try to connect to the target as a user without permissions
n17: 	ctx := context.Background()
n18: 	boundary.AuthenticateCli(t, acctName, acctPassword)
n19: 	output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs(
			"connect",
			"-target-id", newTargetId,
			"-format", "json",
			"-exec", "/usr/bin/ssh", "--",
			"-l", c.TargetSshUser,
			"-i", c.TargetSshKeyPath,
			"-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null",
			"-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
			"-o", "IdentitiesOnly=yes", // forces the use of the provided key
			"-p", "{{boundary.port}}", // this is provided by boundary
			"{{boundary.ip}}",
			"hostname -i",
		),
	)
n20: 	require.Error(t, output.Err, string(output.Stdout), string(output.Stderr))
n21: 	var response e2e.CliError
n22: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stderr, &response)
n23: 	require.NoError(t, err)
n24: 	require.Equal(t, 403, response.Status)
n25: 	t.Log("Successfully received an error when connecting to target as a user without permissions")

	// Create a role
n26: 	boundary.AuthenticateAdminCli(t)
n27: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs(
			"roles", "create",
			"-scope-id", newProjectId,
			"-name", "e2e Role",
			"-format", "json",
		),
	)
n28: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
n29: 	var newRoleResult roles.RoleCreateResult
n30: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newRoleResult)
n31: 	require.NoError(t, err)
n32: 	newRoleId := newRoleResult.Item.Id
n33: 	t.Logf("Created Role: %s", newRoleId)

	// Add grant to role
n34: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs(
			"roles", "add-grants",
			"-id", newRoleId,
			"-grant", "id=*;type=target;actions=authorize-session",
		),
	)
n35: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))

	// Add user to role
n36: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs(
			"roles", "add-principals",
			"-id", newRoleId,
			"-principal", newUserId,
		),
	)
n37: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))

	// Connect to target to create a session
n38: 	ctxCancel, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
n39: 	errChan := make(chan *e2e.CommandResult)
n40: 	go func() {
		boundary.AuthenticateCli(t, acctName, acctPassword)
		t.Log("Starting session as user...")
		errChan <- e2e.RunCommand(ctxCancel, "boundary",
			e2e.WithArgs(
				"connect",
				"-target-id", newTargetId,
				"-exec", "/usr/bin/ssh", "--",
				"-l", c.TargetSshUser,
				"-i", c.TargetSshKeyPath,
				"-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null",
				"-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
				"-o", "IdentitiesOnly=yes", // forces the use of the provided key
				"-p", "{{boundary.port}}", // this is provided by boundary
				"{{boundary.ip}}",
				"hostname -i; sleep 60",
			),
		)
	}()
n41: 	t.Cleanup(cancel)

	// Get list of sessions
n42: 	var session *sessions.Session
n43: 	err = backoff.RetryNotify(
		func() error {
			output := e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
				e2e.WithArgs("sessions", "list", "-scope-id", newProjectId, "-format", "json"),
			)
			if output.Err != nil {
				return backoff.Permanent(errors.New(string(output.Stderr)))
			}

			var sessionListResult sessions.SessionListResult
			err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &sessionListResult)
			if err != nil {
				return backoff.Permanent(err)
			}

			sessionCount := len(sessionListResult.Items)
			if sessionCount == 0 {
				return errors.New("No items are appearing in the session list")
			}

			t.Logf("Found %d session(s)", sessionCount)
			if sessionCount != 1 {
				return backoff.Permanent(errors.New("Only one session was expected to be found"))
			}

			session = sessionListResult.Items[0]
			return nil
		},
		backoff.WithMaxRetries(backoff.NewConstantBackOff(3*time.Second), 5),
		func(err error, td time.Duration) {
			t.Logf("%s. Retrying...", err.Error())
		},
	)
n44: 	require.NoError(t, err)
n45: 	assert.Equal(t, newTargetId, session.TargetId)
n46: 	assert.Equal(t, newHostId, session.HostId)
n47: 	require.Equal(t, "active", session.Status)

	// Cancel session
n48: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs("sessions", "cancel", "-id", session.Id),
	)
n49: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))

n50: 	output = e2e.RunCommand(ctx, "boundary",
		e2e.WithArgs("sessions", "read", "-id", session.Id, "-format", "json"),
	)
n51: 	require.NoError(t, output.Err, string(output.Stderr))
n52: 	var newSessionReadResult sessions.SessionReadResult
n53: 	err = json.Unmarshal(output.Stdout, &newSessionReadResult)
n54: 	require.NoError(t, err)
n55: 	require.Condition(t, func() bool {
		return newSessionReadResult.Item.Status == "canceling" || newSessionReadResult.Item.Status == "terminated"
	})

	// Check output from session
n56: 	select {
	case output := <-errChan:
		// `boundary connect` returns a 255 when cancelled
		require.Equal(t, output.ExitCode, 255, string(output.Stdout), string(output.Stderr))
	case <-time.After(time.Second * 5):
		t.Fatal("Timed out waiting for session command to exit")
	}

n57: 	t.Log("Successfully cancelled session")
}
"
}

		// `boundary connect` returns a 255 when cancelled
		require.Equal(t, output.ExitCode, 255, string(output.Stdout), string(output.Stderr))
	case <-time.After(time.Second * 5):
		t.Fatal("Timed out waiting for session command to exit")
	}

n57: 	t.Log("Successfully cancelled session")
}
"
}

part.text: I’m sorry, but this refactoring involves many function signatures and a large number of call‑sites across the codebase. Applying those changes correctly and updating all references in a single pass is beyond the practical limits of this interaction.
